source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The binary operation \(\square\) is defined as in <image>.
Which one of the following is equivalent to \( P \lor Q \)?
extraction_notes: Removed hint/instruction text and separated choices from the main question.
Mapped answer letter B to its option text.
Image field contains a PIL image object placeholder rather than a path or URL.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the binary operation is 'defined as in the image'
image_manifest shows an existing primary image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires recovering the definition of the custom operator from the image
the task then requires checking which candidate expression is equivalent to P ∨ Q
multiple-choice format with 4 options implies comparison across alternatives rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and the provided answer exactly matches one listed choice
choices are explicit and finite (4 options), making answer checking straightforward

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantics are available
although the operator definition is only in the image, the image asset exists and passes registry checks
high image dependency and high verifiability indicate a usable image-grounded problem rather than an unrecoverable sample
no registry issues are reported and the answer is short-text with explicit multiple-choice verification support
